entries.statement: Knowledge of data modeling and schema design techniques, including conceptual, logical, and physical modeling approaches, entity-relationship diagrams, dimensional modeling approaches for analytics, and design trade-offs between normalization and query performance.

Knowledge of data platform architecture patterns including data warehouse, data lake, data lakehouse, data mesh, and data fabric architectures, and the trade-offs and selection criteria for each pattern.
Knowledge of complex data structures and types, including nested structures, array types, graph data structures, and their capabilities and applications within data systems.
Knowledge of data integration and ETL patterns, including ETL, ELT, change data capture (CDC), event streaming, API-based integration, and data virtualization approaches.
Knowledge of data processing and transformation techniques, including data manipulation, mapping, format conversion, cleansing, data fusion, and aggregation methods.

Knowledge of semantic layer and metrics layer design, including measure definition, dimension hierarchy design, slowly changing dimension handling, metric definition standards, and centralized metric consistency across reporting tools.
Knowledge of distributed data processing frameworks (e.g., Spark, Flink, Beam) and the decision criteria for selecting each framework based on data volume, latency requirements, and complexity needs.

Knowledge of data pipeline orchestration and scheduling, including orchestration tools (e.g., Airflow, Prefect, Dagster), DAG design for observability and recoverability, dependency management, failure handling, retry strategies, idempotency, and SLA-driven monitoring.

Knowledge of dbt and transformation framework patterns, including model layering strategies (staging, intermediate, mart layers), testing approaches, documentation generation, and automated lineage creation.
Knowledge of infrastructure-as-code principles and tooling (e.g., Terraform, Pulumi) as applied to data platform provisioning, configuration management, reproducibility, and version control.
Knowledge of data platform security controls, including network isolation, encryption at rest and in transit, identity and access management, audit logging, and secure architecture patterns.

Knowledge of data privacy and protection techniques as applied to data architecture, including data masking, tokenization, anonymization, pseudonymization, and privacy-by-design architectural patterns.

Knowledge of data pipeline monitoring and observability, including data freshness tracking, volume anomaly detection, schema change detection, job failure pattern analysis, and alerting system design.

Knowledge of data classification, cataloging, and metadata management systems, including automated data discovery, business glossary integration, metadata standards, and the role of metadata in enabling governance and self-service consumption.

Knowledge of database administration and lifecycle management, including database provisioning, configuration, maintenance, performance monitoring, patching, version upgrades, and end-of-life migration planning.

Knowledge of data backup, recovery, and disaster recovery strategies, including backup scheduling and retention policies, recovery point and recovery time objectives (RPO/RTO), and recoverability testing procedures.

Knowledge of data lineage and provenance tracking systems, including automated lineage capture, column-level lineage, impact analysis, and how lineage supports regulatory compliance, debugging, and data asset trust.

Knowledge of API design and data service patterns, including RESTful and GraphQL API design, API gateway architecture, rate limiting, versioning strategies, and the role of APIs in enabling controlled and scalable data consumption.

Knowledge of event-driven architecture patterns for data systems, including message brokers, event sourcing, command-query responsibility segregation (CQRS), publish-subscribe models, and trade-offs between event-driven and request-response integration.

Knowledge of risk management frameworks and processes for data systems, including risk assessment methodologies, risk models and frameworks, risk scoring, and supply chain risk management for data platform vendors.

Knowledge of risk mitigation and compliance within data architectures, including risk mitigation strategies, residual risk assessment, compliance documentation, risk acceptance, and risk tolerance principles.

Knowledge of DataOps and CI/CD practices for data systems, including continuous integration and continuous deployment for data pipelines, automated testing of transformations, and the operational disciplines that enable reliable data delivery.

Knowledge of container orchestration and cloud-native data infrastructure, including Kubernetes-based data workloads, serverless compute for data processing, and trade-offs between managed services and self-hosted infrastructure.

Knowledge of cloud cost optimization and FinOps principles for data platforms, including cloud cost models and pricing structures, reserved versus on-demand capacity trade-offs, cost allocation methods, and optimization strategies for data workloads.

Knowledge of schema evolution and change management practices for data systems, including backward and forward compatibility strategies, schema registry patterns, migration strategies, and governance processes that control schema changes across environments.

Knowledge of data platform capacity planning and performance engineering, including workload characterization, resource utilization analysis, scaling strategies, and the relationship between architecture decisions and system performance under load.

Knowledge of system availability, reliability, and fault tolerance principles for data platforms, including availability metrics and requirements, fault tolerance mechanisms, system resilience strategies, and performance under load conditions.
